corpus: Plant near-miss and incident register (synthetic, 24 months)
note: Synthetic records consistent with the modelled plant. Contributing-factor taxonomy follows the categories used in published coke-oven incident investigations.

records.title: Crew withdrew from Battery 3 basement after CO rose during standpipe work
records.outcome: No injury. Crew self-evacuated on personal monitor alarm.
records.note: Gas-main isolation had not been applied; entry proceeded on an entrance-only gas test.

records.title: Regulator PR-102 found passing during routine round
records.note: Riser pressure had been drifting for several shifts below the warning limit.

records.title: Hot work started 15 m from an open basement vent
records.outcome: Stopped by shift supervisor before ignition source was struck.
records.note: Two permits were active in adjacent zones with no cross-check between them.

records.title: Single-point gas test passed; rear of chamber later found at 60 ppm CO
records.note: Test at the entrance is not representative of the chamber volume.

records.title: Night-shift pressure excursion not carried into the morning handover
records.outcome: Detected on the next round. No exposure.
records.note: Excursion was logged and cleared; the trend was never reviewed.

records.title: Confined-space entry began before isolation certificate was countersigned
records.note: Isolation was physically applied 40 minutes after entry.

records.title: H2S exposure during by-products line breaking
records.outcome: One worker treated for exposure; returned to duty.
records.note: Line was believed drained; residual liquor remained.

records.title: Valve throttling during chamber work raised back-pressure on an adjacent riser
records.outcome: Noticed on the trend; valve reopened.
records.note: Isolation valve was throttled to support unrelated maintenance.

records.title: CO reading dismissed as sensor drift; adjacent channel later confirmed the rise
records.note: A second channel in the same zone corroborated the rise 20 minutes later.

records.title: Two permits active in connected zones without cross-reference
records.note: Neither permit holder was aware of the other.

records.title: December audit observation on regulator PR-104 deferred twice
records.outcome: Scheduled under a later work order.
records.note: Backlog item carried across two planning cycles.

records.title: Entry crew relied on a gas test taken 90 minutes earlier
records.note: Conditions in a coke-oven basement can change within minutes.

factor_labels.isolation_omitted: Gas-main isolation not applied before confined-space entry
factor_labels.confined_space_entry: Confined-space entry in a gas-bearing area
factor_labels.single_point_gas_test: Gas test taken at a single point, not representative of the volume
factor_labels.stale_gas_test: Gas test not repeated immediately before entry
factor_labels.sub_threshold_drift: Sustained parameter drift below the alarm limit
factor_labels.gas_trend_ignored: Rising gas trend attributed to instrument error
factor_labels.single_channel_reliance: Decision taken on one channel without corroboration
factor_labels.handover_gap: Unresolved condition not carried across a shift changeover
factor_labels.alarm_acknowledged_without_review: Excursion acknowledged and cleared without trend review
factor_labels.hot_work_proximity: Hot work adjacent to a gas-bearing or vented area
factor_labels.permit_overlap: Simultaneous permits in connected zones without cross-check
factor_labels.permit_discipline: Permit sequence not followed as written
factor_labels.valve_operation_uncoordinated: Valve operation not coordinated with concurrent work
factor_labels.back_pressure: Back-pressure raised on a connected leg
factor_labels.deferred_maintenance: Known defect deferred across planning cycles
factor_labels.ppe_lapse: Personal protective equipment not used as specified
